# Pr 91734 Deprecate Old Capability Schema Add Runtime Hints And Function Call Credits

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_cc9fd576760d4a058a896155657eff06
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
Deprecate legacy capability schema; add runtime hint headers, function-call credits, and streaming degradation policy

Motivation: We have multiple downstream needs that couldn't be satisfied by the legacy model capability schema: (1) lightweight, per-request runtime hints that the routing layer and serving runtime can use without deserializing the full model profile; (2) a transparent metering knob for tool/function calling (customers asked for per-invocation crediting separate from token billing); and (3) richer guidance for streaming fallbacks so clients can degrade more gracefully when a model variant switches to a lower-throughput tier. This PR introduces three related surfaces: capability_headers, function_call_credit, and streaming_degradation_policy, alongside an OpenAI-compatible shim that surfaces structured-output hints in /v1/models list responses.

Summary of changes (high level):
- API: Add optional capability_headers map to /v1/models and /v1/models/:id responses. These are short key/value runtime hints intended for fast routing decisions (examples: "supports_prefix_cache": "true", "max_prefetch_tokens": "256").
- Catalog schema: Deprecate legacy capability_schema.v1 (kept as legacy_capabilities for migration) and introduce capability_schema.v2 that references compact headers + full capability document. Migration docs added.
- Billing: Introduce function_call_credit as a first-class optional numeric field on model profiles and endpoints. This allows the billing system to attribute cost for tool/function call ops independently; SDKs and console will display credits usage.
- Streaming: Add streaming_degradation_policy on model profiles to expose a list of ordered fallbacks and suggested partial-output tokens thresholds (helps clients decide when to switch to a summarized streaming path). Also include preferred_prefetch_hint to suggest prefix caching window.
- OpenAI compat: Update the /v1/models listing returned by the OpenAI-compat shim to include capability_headers and structured_output_hints under the "capabilities" umbrella so downstream OpenAI-compatible clients can discover behavior without touching Redwood-specific endpoints.
- Docs & SDK: Docs page describing migration, examples for reading capability_headers and consuming streaming_degradation_policy; Python and TypeScript SDK surfaces to parse new fields.

Commits (summary):
- commit 1: api: extend model list + get handlers to populate capability_headers and streaming_degradation_policy (handlers and serializers)
- commit 2: catalog: add models_v2 schema, migration script to copy legacy_capabilities into full_profile and generate default headers
- commit 3: billing: record function_call_credit into usage events and expose in billing UI (console changes)
- commit 4: sdk/docs: parse helpers and docs for migration and examples

Files changed (representative):
- src/api/handlers/models_list.go (updated serializer and OpenAI-compat shim)
- src/catalog/schemas/models_v2.json (new compact schema)
- tools/migrations/migrate_capabilities_v1_to_v2.go (migration script)
- services/billing/credits.go (add function_call_credit aggregation)
- console/src/components/model/ModelDetails.tsx (show capability headers and credits)
- sdk/python/redwood/models.py (new fields + helper parser)
- docs/migrations/capability_headers_migration.md (migration and rollback guide)

Testing and validation:
- Unit tests: added for serializer and parser behavior (capability_headers omitted vs present), billing aggregation tests for function_call_credit.
- Integration: end-to-end test in perf-canary that validates streaming_degradation_policy is honored by the router under simulated capacity pressure; canary shows no increase in 95p latency on main model variants.
- Backwards compatibility: /v1/models still returns legacy_capabilities field for consumers that haven't migrated; the OpenAI-compat shim prefers capability_headers but will fall back to legacy_capabilities when capability_headers is absent.

Migration strategy & rollout:
- Stage 1 (canary): populate capability_headers from existing full capabilities via migration script; run canary for 2 days to ensure no regression in routing or billing.
- Stage 2 (gradual): expose new headers to 20% of API responses; monitor routing decisions and SDK parsing errors.
- Stage 3 (opt-in enforcement): allow customers to opt-in to strict schema validation via a console toggle (default off).
- Final: Deprecate legacy schema via announcement; grace period until 2026-06-01, after which legacy_capabilities may be removed for new write operations.

Review conversation (condensed):
- Owen Park: "Large change — please ensure migration script is idempotent and add a safety flag so we can revert on dataset problems." — Author: "Migration is idempotent; added --dry-run and --cutover flags in migrate_capabilities_v1_to_v2.go; documented in migration md."
- Lina Gomez: "Need to ensure function_call_credit can't be negative and that default is 0 where absent; also clarify how it interacts with token billing." — Author: "Validation added; billing pipeline will emit both tokens and function_call_credit events. Release notes updated to clarify pricing semantics."
- Harriet Zhao: "Does the streaming_degradation_policy open us up to oscillation if multiple routers act on hints?" — Author: "Router change includes a small randomized jitter and a dead-time to prevent oscillation; added comment in routing code and unit test."
- QA (Slack): reported one flaky test in console snapshot related to ModelDetails layout — fixed by stabilizing test data ordering in the SDK.

CI summary: initial run had lint warnings (long line in models_list.go) and one flaky integration test; both addressed in follow-up commits. Final status: all checks passed (unit, lint, integration, e2e canary). Performance canary showed stable tail latency; billing integration tests validated function_call_credit emitted for simulated tool calls.

Rollout notes for operators:
- The migration tool writes a migration_log to S3; if any migration errors occur, the --rollback flag will reverse schema updates made to the catalog DB.
- Feature gate: runtime.capability_headers (default: enabled for all API nodes in next release). If issues appear, ops can disable gate per region to return legacy behavior immediately.

Decision rationale and follow-ups:
- We intentionally kept capability_headers compact and stringly-typed to avoid heavyweight schema churn; full capabilities remain available for deep inspection and compatibility with existing tooling.
- Follow-ups: ENG-2639 to add UI visualization for streaming_degradation_policy, ENG-2670 to instrument per-function-call credit telemetry in the billing dashboard, and ENG-2692 to add a half-life cache invalidation for capability_headers to support fast rollbacks.

Merge outcome: Squash merge by Lina Gomez on 2026-02-19 after approvals from all reviewers and green CI. Release note entry drafted and scheduled for the 2026-02 minor release.
Add new capability header fields (capability_headers) and introduce function_call_credit metric for per-call accounting; deprecate legacy capability schema v1 with migration path; add streaming degradation policy and prefetch hint support. Non-breaking for read-only clients; write-migration recommended before 2026-06-01.
